notes: This batch continues the post-closure residual paired-family campaign.
The family is bounded to the current topology4 runner plus the repo-held admitted terrain8 surface attributed to the same runner hash.
The runner's own local channelfamily result stays comparison-only because preserving the seam is the point of the batch.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Current local result surface emitted by the runner's present-day output contract; used to preserve the schema mismatch.
Shows both the current channelfamily naming and the admitted terrain8 artifact in the same topology4 strip.
Directly admits the selected terrain8 surface under the current runner hash.
Provides residual campaign continuity and identifies this seam as the next prioritized unit.

runtime_expectations: The current runner writes results_axis12_topology4_channelfamily_suite_v2.json and one local SIM_EVIDENCE block for S_SIM_AXIS12_TOPOLOGY4_CHANNELFAMILY_SUITE_V2.
The selected admitted terrain8 surface is repo-held and evidence-pack admitted under the same runner hash but a different SIM_ID.
If residual pair intake continues, the next prioritized pair begins at run_axis12_topology4_channelgrid_v1.py.
failure_modes: Smoothing the admitted terrain8 surface into the current local topology4-family contract because the hash matches.
Ignoring the runner's own local result file and losing the seam entirely.
Assuming exact repo-top admission means the current runner still emits the admitted surface.
Collapsing the next topology4 channelgrid pair into the current batch.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
